Activity Name: Intern Summer Justice Program – Law Office of the Public Defender
Description: The Intern Summer Justice Program at the Law Office of the Public Defender offers a unique two-week internship for high school students interested in the justice system. This program, designed specifically for students aged 15 to 18 with a GPA of 2.0 or higher, provides an immersive experience into the workings of the criminal justice courts, law enforcement, crime lab, and medical examiner’s office. Participants will shadow attorneys, observe court proceedings, engage with legal professionals, and conclude with a mock trial. The program runs Monday through Friday, 9:00 am – 3:00 pm, for 10 consecutive business days and is open to students who have completed 9th grade in Broward County, Florida.
State: Florida

The challenge is open to students from participating districts, offering them a platform to showcase their apps and potentially win awards. This initiative not only promotes STEM education but also aims to reach underserved, diverse, and rural student populations, making it an inclusive opportunity for students interested in computer science.

Both tracks include workshops to build academic and professional skills, and the program culminates in a Poster Symposium where students present their research findings. Description: The INSIGHT High School Program at the Harborview Injury Prevention & Research Center is a four-week virtual program designed to introduce high school students to public health, biomedical research, and medicine with a focus on injury and violence prevention. Description: The United States Senate Youth Program (USSYP) is a prestigious opportunity for high school students interested in public service, leadership, and government. Participants engage with senators, cabinet members, and officials from various federal agencies. Highlights include meetings with the president and a U.S. Supreme Court justice. Description: The Engineering & Computer Science Research Internships at Syracuse University is a 6-week non-residential summer program for rising 12th grade students in the greater Syracuse area.
